explanations.3: ARG lets you define variables that can be passed when building the image, like a function parameter for docker build.
explanations.10: The `-m 0700` sets restrictive permissions (only owner can read/write) on the .ssh folder for security, and ssh-keyscan pre-populates known_hosts so git doesn't prompt you about trusting github.com.
explanations.13: The `--mount=type=secret` temporarily mounts your SSH key into the container during build without baking it into the final image, keeping it secret.
explanations.15: The backslash at the end of line 13 continues the RUN command onto the next line; this typo `cd app` should be `cd /app` or the git clone will go to the wrong directory.
explanations.17: The `|| true` at the end means 'ignore errors' — the SSH test to github.com might fail but we still want the build to continue.
explanations.24: This keeps the container running indefinitely by tailing an empty file, useful for containers you want to stay alive for debugging or manual commands.
